AssumeRole.document:
This API is used to request temporary access credentials for assuming a role.

This API is used to describe the composition of role policies.

This API is used to specify the role trust policy, which defines who can assume the role.

This API is used to specify the role permission policy, which defines what actions can be performed after assuming the role.


This API is used to describe the conditions under which a role can be assumed.

This API is used to bind a policy that allows a user to call AssumeRole.

This API is used to add a user as a principal in the role trust policy.
AssumeRole.name: Applies for temporary access credentials to assume a role

AssumeRoleWithSAML.document:
This API is used to request temporary access credentials for a role based on a SAML assertion.

Note: When called with signature method v3, header `X-TC-Token` could be ignored, and `Authorization` should be SKIP.
AssumeRoleWithSAML.name: Requests temporary access credentials for a role based on a SAML assertion

AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity.document:
This API is used to apply for an OIDC role credential.

Note: When called with signature method v3, header `X-TC-Token` could be ignored, and `Authorization` should be SKIP.
AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity.name: Applies for an OIDC role credential

GetCallerIdentity.document:
This API is used to get the identity information of the current caller.

This API is used to get the identity information using the persistent keys of the root account and sub-account as well as the temporary credentials generated by AssumeRole and GetFederationToken.
GetCallerIdentity.name: Gets the identity information of the current caller

GetFederationToken.document:
This API is used to provide instructions.

This API is used to return a set of temporary access credentials. A typical application scenario is for proxy applications to centrally request temporary access credentials and distribute them to other distributed terminal applications within the enterprise network, such as terminal applications uploading files to COS. This API only supports calls with permanent keys.

This API is used to implement best practices.

1. This API is used to recommend reusing temporary access credentials within their validity period to avoid rate limiting due to increased business request rates.
2. This API is used to recommend granting temporary access credentials with the principle of least privilege.
3. This API is used to recommend not using the root account for permanent keys calling the API.
GetFederationToken.name: Gets temporary credentials for federated user

members.document:
Resource descriptions of a role, which can be obtained by clicking the role name in the [CAM console](https://console.cloud.tencent.com/cam/role).
General role:
qcs::cam::uin/12345678:role/4611686018427397919, qcs::cam::uin/12345678:roleName/testRoleName
Service role:
qcs::cam::uin/12345678:role/tencentcloudServiceRole/4611686018427397920, qcs::cam::uin/12345678:role/tencentcloudServiceRoleName/testServiceRoleName
members.name: RoleArn

members.document:
User-defined temporary session name.
It can contain 2-128 letters, digits, and symbols (=,.@_-). Regex: [\w+=,.@_-]*
members.name: RoleSessionName

members.document: Specifies the validity period of the temporary access credentials in seconds. Default value: 7,200s. Maximum value: 43,200s.
members.name: DurationSeconds

members.document:
Policy description

Note:

This parameter needs to be URL-encoded. The server will URL-decode this field and grant temporary access credentials based on the processed policy. Please pass the parameter according to the specification. (If you request a TencentCloud API through the GET method, all parameters must be URL-encoded again in accordance with [Signature v3](https://intl.cloud.tencent.com/document/api/598/33159?from_cn_redirect=1#1.-.E6.8B.BC.E6.8E.A5.E8.A7.84.E8.8C.83.E8.AF.B7.E6.B1.82.E4.B8.B2) before the request is sent).
The policy syntax refers to [CAM's Syntax Logic](https://intl.cloud.tencent.com/document/product/598/10603?from_cn_redirect=1).
3. The policy cannot contain the `principal` element.
members.name: Policy

members.document:
External role ID, which can be obtained by clicking the role name in the [CAM console](https://console.cloud.tencent.com/cam/role).
It can contain 2-128 letters, digits, and symbols (=,.@:/-). Regex: [\w+=,.@:\/-]*
members.name: ExternalId

members.document: List of session tags. Up to 50 tags are allowed. The tag keys can not duplicate.

members.document: Token, which contains up to 4,096 bytes depending on the associated policies.

members.document: Temporary credentials key ID, which contains up to 1,024 bytes.
members.name: TmpSecretId

members.document: Temporary credentials key, which contains up to 1,024 bytes.
members.name: TmpSecretKey

members.document: ARN of the current caller.
members.name: Arn

members.document: Root account UIN of the current caller.
members.name: AccountId

members.document:
User ID.
1. If the caller is a Tencent Cloud account, the UIN of the current account is returned.
2. If the caller is a role, `roleId:roleSessionName` is returned.
3. If the caller is a federated user, `uin:federatedUserName` is returned.
members.name: UserId

members.document:
Account UIN.
1. If the caller is a Tencent Cloud account, the UIN of the current account is returned.
2. If the caller is a role, the UIN of the account that applies for the role is returned.
members.name: PrincipalId

members.document: Identity type.
members.name: Type

members.document: The unique request ID, generated by the server, will be returned for every request (if the request fails to reach the server for other reasons, the request will not obtain a RequestId). RequestId is required for locating a problem.
members.name: RequestId

members.document: The customizable name of the caller, consisting of letters
members.name: Name

members.document:
Note:

The policy syntax refers to [CAM's Syntax Logic](https://intl.cloud.tencent.com/document/product/598/10603?from_cn_redirect=1).
The policy cannot contain the principal element.
This parameter needs to be URL-encoded. The server will URL-decode this field and grant temporary access credentials based on the processed policy. Please pass the parameter according to the specification.
members.name: Policy

members.document: The validity period of temporary credentials in seconds. Default value: 1,800s. Maximum value for a root account: 7,200s. Maximum value for a sub-account: 129,600s.
members.name: DurationSeconds

members.document: Temporary access credentials
members.name: Credentials

members.document: Temporary access credentials expiration time. A Unix timestamp will be returned which is accurate to the second.
members.name: ExpiredTime

members.document:
Temporary access credentials expiration time in UTC time in ISO 8601 format.
Note: this field may return null, indicating that no valid values can be obtained.
members.name: Expiration

members.document: The unique request ID, generated by the server, will be returned for every request (if the request fails to reach the server for other reasons, the request will not obtain a RequestId). RequestId is required for locating a problem.
members.name: RequestId

members.document: Tag key. It’s up to 128 characters and case-sensitive.
members.name: Key

members.document: Tag value. It’s up to 256 characters and case-sensitive.
members.name: Value
